lint.command: cd /Volumes/DPC/work/plant-code/plant3d-web/.worktrees/mission-review-m1-m2 && npx eslint src/components/review/ReviewerTaskList.vue src/components/review/WorkflowSubmitDialog.vue src/components/review/WorkflowReturnDialog.vue src/components/review/ReviewPanel.vue src/composables/useReviewStore.ts src/composables/useUserStore.ts src/api/reviewApi.ts

blockingIssues.description: `handleReturnToNode()` always calls `userStore.returnTaskToNode(currentTask.value.id, 'sj', ...)`, and the dialog confirm handler at line 829 discards the emitted `_targetNode`. That means the new standard return dialog never returns to the user-selected previous node, despite `WorkflowReturnDialog` advertising selectable prior nodes and the feature description promising standard dialog semantics without hidden legacy behavior. From `sh` or `pz`, the UI suggests the reviewer can step back one stage, but the code always jumps all the way to design.

The new smoke-path test only asserts panel command ordering plus unchanged fixture fields, but never proves that reviewer entry reaches an active task or that the active task preserves shared context after hydration. Because `setCurrentTask` is mocked as a no-op, the test cannot catch regressions where the actual workbench hydration drops `formId`, attachments, or workflow history, so it does not cover the feature's required app-entry -> reviewer active task evidence.

The updated M2 documentation now describes an app-entry -> reviewer inbox -> active task -> workflow action smoke path as shipped behavior, but the worker's own browser evidence recorded only an empty inbox (`暂无审核任务`) and explicitly states no real task could be opened or action executed. This is a contract mismatch: the doc presents a completed cross-surface path that was not actually demonstrated in the local smoke evidence.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The worker marked `followedProcedure` true even though the skill requires manual validation of a reviewer task selection path, but the transcript and handoff only closed a reserved browser session and recorded no live reviewer smoke flow.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `/Users/dongpengcheng/.factory/missions/4d7bd249-b8ec-4d5d-9033-77ef2ded8396/handoffs/2026-03-13T14-51-04-948Z__m2-reviewer-inbox-and-task-hydration__41dae33f-60da-4cb3-a29b-b0713f26a591.json` shows `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` while `verification.interactiveChecks[0].observed` says no live browser-based reviewer smoke flow was started; `review-entry-worker` step 9 requires selecting a task and validating the reviewer-facing path on `3101`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The `review-entry-worker` skill requires live reviewer browser smoke validation in step 9, but this worker still returned success after skipping it because port `3101` might belong to another checkout. The skill should either define an explicit fallback/return-to-orchestrator rule for this mission constraint or the mission docs should say automated-only completion is acceptable in that case.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: .factory/skills/review-entry-worker/SKILL.md step 9 requires opening `http://127.0.0.1:3101/` and validating a task/action path, while the handoff for session `87cd126c-0ca3-403d-9f9a-0cc05c58a550` records `followedProcedure: false` and says browser smoke was skipped due to unrelated worktree changes / possible shared 3101 ownership. `AGENTS.md` also lists this as a return-to-orchestrator condition.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The `review-entry-worker` skill requires validating at least one task selection plus one workflow action path, but it does not say what to do when the local dataset has no reviewer-visible task. This worker marked the procedure as fully followed despite stopping at the empty inbox, so the skill should explicitly require returning a blocked outcome or capturing a readiness gap when step 9 cannot reach an active reviewer task.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: .factory/skills/review-entry-worker/SKILL.md step 9 requires `validate at least one task selection plus one workflow action path`; handoff `2026-03-13T15-07-24-916Z__m2-cross-surface-smoke-path__d0929d4f-2663-4af7-874c-29c39a1067e7.json` says no reviewer-visible task was available yet `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` stayed true.

rejectedObservations.observation: Mission shared state still claims `GET /api/review/tasks` returned live review task data during planning, but both the earlier handoff-contract worker and this feature reported no usable reviewer-visible task data for live cross-surface validation. The environment notes should distinguish generic backend reachability from seeded reviewer-task readiness so workers do not assume INT-008 can be completed without extra setup.
rejectedObservations.reason: shared library note may be outdated, but this validator does not have enough independent evidence to safely edit .factory/library without reconciling conflicting mission history.
